items.title: I enjoy offseason rotation prediction stuff.
items.author: Law Murray 📘
items.body_excerpt:
I enjoy offseason rotation prediction stuff. 

Seeing a lot of Keaton Wagler automatic starter stuff

For a team that has some interest in defending...

I will point out that Kris Dunn and/or Derrick Jones Jr. have started 172 out of 172 games since they joined LAC (including playoffs/Play-In)

items.title: Another strong game for Luka Garza today as Bosnia and Herzegovina fell to Serbia in a FIBA World Cup qualifier.
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
Another strong game for Luka Garza today as Bosnia and Herzegovina fell to Serbia in a FIBA World Cup qualifier.

Garza had 16 points and 7 rebounds.

Nikola Jokic went for 20/10/11. Nikola Jovic went off for 32 points on 11-for-15 shooting (7-for-9 3Ps), while adding 3 blocks.

items.body_excerpt:
ICYMI: In May 2024, Joel Embiid talked at length about the Sixers needing to finally build continuity with one consistent group. He wanted to be like Jayson Tatum and Jaylen Brown.

That summer, the Sixers signed Paul George.

Two years later…

items.title: Hyunjung Lee is widely regarded as the top basketball player in South Korea.
items.author: Jeff McDonald
items.body_excerpt:
Hyunjung Lee is widely regarded as the top basketball player in South Korea. 

So why is he at Summer League with the Spurs while his national team is playing in World Cup qualifiers on the other side of an ocean?

"I have to chase my dream,” he said.

www.expressnews.com/sports/spurs...

media.title: Why the 'Ace of South Korea' is summering with the Spurs
media.description: South Korean star Hyunjung Lee gave up a chance to play for his national team this month to spend it in Summer League with the Spurs

items.title: Hawks have 3 roster bigs in Summer League and they can all play together in pairs.
items.author: Brad Rowland
items.body_excerpt:
Hawks have 3 roster bigs in Summer League and they can all play together in pairs.

Makes it easy to get them all a bunch of quality minutes.

items.title: Canada drew about 11,000 for its FIBA World Cup qualifier on Friday and it looks like as many tonight here in Hamilton against Jamaica.
items.author: Doug Smith
items.body_excerpt:
Canada drew about 11,000 for its FIBA World Cup qualifier on Friday and it looks like as many tonight here in Hamilton against Jamaica.
They are, by a large measure, the biggest crowds to see Canada play at home in more than 30 years. The 1994 worlds might have drawn bigger but not by a lot.

items.title: Spurs officially announced the signing of veteran forward Tobias Harris on Monday, the first day for NBA teams to finalize free agent deals.
items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt:
Spurs officially announced the signing of veteran forward Tobias Harris on Monday, the first day for NBA teams to finalize free agent deals.

The 15-year veteran will wear No. 23.
